ShortDescription.Value: A coverage-gated city levy: fire, police and disaster bill occupants by rent; garbage bills by rent plus a pollution surcharge (polluter pays). Only served buildings are charged.

Publish.LongDescription:
Emergency services and garbage collection are essentially free in vanilla Cities: Skylines II beyond their upkeep. The Service & Sanitation Levy turns them into a fair, coverage-gated revenue stream — like a property tax that only bills the buildings you actually serve.
- Fire, police & disaster: each covered building pays a small % of its own monthly rent, per service. Rent already encodes property value, density and zone type, so the burden stays fair as your city grows. A building not covered for a service isn't billed for it.
- Garbage (sanitation): billed as a % of rent plus a pollution surcharge, so dirty industry pays extra — "polluter pays". Gated on having at least one garbage facility in the city (recycling centres count). The native garbage fee is folded into this levy, so you set it in one place.
- Collected as real city income, straight from occupants' households and companies to the treasury. No renter in a building means no bill.
Opt-out: it ships on with modest starting rates (a few % of city revenue). Turn it off for exact vanilla behaviour, or tune the two rates to taste.
This mod is one of three that replace the older all-in-one "Economy Tweaks" mod (the others are "Welfare Management" and "Private Schools & Hospitals"). Please don't run Economy Tweaks alongside these.
Source (open source, MIT): https://github.com/AmicusDeus/ServiceSanitationLevy
Made with Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool.
